steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: On the left is an independent clause: “These are some of the oldest” (subject + verb). On the right is a prepositional phrase: “of pottery ever found,” which modifies the noun “pieces.” The whole sentence reads as one grammatical unit.

steps.title: Apply the boundary rule
steps.content: A noun followed directly by a modifying prepositional phrase requires no punctuation separating them. A colon would incorrectly introduce an elaboration, a comma would create an unnecessary pause, and an em dash would suggest emphasis or parenthetical content. That matches choice C — C is correct.
